title: endiandra bessaphila
intro: Endiandra bessaphila is a type of plant species, also known as the "Queensland walnut" due to its edible seeds, which are native to Australia. The plant's leaves and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Endiandra bessaphila include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Endiandra bessaphila can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its seeds can also be consumed raw or roasted, although proper preparation and dosing are essential to avoid adverse effects.
